title: pogostemon cyprianii
intro: Pogostemon cyprianii, also known as Patchouli, is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its essential oil's high content of patchouli alcohol and patchoulol. The health benefits of Patchouli include its ability to improve digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia. In herbal preparations, Patchouli is often used in teas, tinctures, and topical creams, and is also used in aromatherapy due to its distinct earthy and herbaceous scent.
